questions.question: How many links make up the 2025 AHA universal Chain of Survival?
options: A. Four
B. Five
C. Six
D. Seven
questions.rationale: The 2025 Guidelines consolidated the separate adult/pediatric and in-hospital/out-of-hospital chains into a single universal six-link Chain of Survival. A separate seven-step-style Neonatal Chain of Care exists for newborns, but the universal chain itself has six links.

questions.question: Which change did the 2025 AHA Guidelines make to the Chain of Survival?
options: A. Removed the defibrillation link
B. Consolidated the separate chains into one universal chain and added a distinct newborn chain
C. Reduced the number of links to four
D. Eliminated the recovery link
questions.rationale: The 2025 update merged the previously separate adult, pediatric, in-hospital, and out-of-hospital chains into a single universal Chain of Survival, and introduced a separate Neonatal Chain of Care for newborns.

questions.question: Which two interventions, both within BLS scope, are most strongly associated with survival from cardiac arrest?
options: A. Advanced airway placement and epinephrine
B. Early high-quality CPR and rapid defibrillation
C. IV access and 12-lead ECG
D. Targeted temperature management and coronary angiography
questions.rationale: Early bystander CPR and rapid defibrillation are the interventions most strongly linked to survival, and both are core BLS actions available before advanced providers arrive.

questions.question: In the in-hospital cardiac arrest (IHCA) setting, which emphasis is placed at the front of the system of care?
options: A. Public-access AED programs
B. Surveillance and prevention with rapid-response systems
C. Bystander recognition by untrained laypeople
D. Dispatcher-assisted CPR
questions.rationale: In hospitals, surveillance, monitoring, and rapid-response systems aim to recognize deterioration early and prevent many arrests before they occur, unlike the bystander-dependent front of out-of-hospital arrest.

questions.question: What does the recovery link of the Chain of Survival address?
options: A. Only the immediate minutes after ROSC in the field
B. Physical, cognitive, and emotional rehabilitation and support for survivors and families after arrest
C. Replacing defibrillator pads after use
D. Documentation and billing for the resuscitation
questions.rationale: The recovery link recognizes that survival continues beyond return of a pulse; survivors may need rehabilitation, psychological support, and treatment planning, and families need support as well.

questions.question: A bystander calls 911 and the dispatcher coaches them to push hard and fast on the chest. This is an example of:
options: A. Advanced cardiac life support
B. Dispatcher-assisted CPR
C. Post-cardiac arrest care
D. The recovery link
questions.rationale: Dispatcher-assisted CPR is when emergency dispatchers coach bystanders over the phone to recognize arrest and begin chest compressions before EMS arrives — a key part of the system of care.

questions.question: Why is the 'weakest link' concept important in the Chain of Survival?
options: A. Because only one link needs to work for survival
B. Because a delay or failure in any single link reduces the effectiveness of the entire chain
C. Because advanced care can always compensate for delayed CPR
D. Because the links can be performed in any order
questions.rationale: Each link depends on the ones before it. A weak or delayed link — such as late recognition — cannot be fully rescued by strong later care, so speed and quality matter at every step.

questions.question: Which of the following is the FIRST link of the universal Chain of Survival?
options: A. Defibrillation
B. High-quality CPR
C. Activation of the emergency response
D. Post-cardiac arrest care
questions.rationale: Activation of the emergency response is the first link — without calling for help and summoning an AED and advanced providers, the later links cannot occur.

questions.question: Roughly what happens to survival probability for each minute defibrillation is delayed in a shockable arrest without CPR?
options: A. It increases
B. It stays the same
C. It falls substantially
D. It is unaffected by time
questions.rationale: In a shockable rhythm, survival probability declines substantially with each minute defibrillation is delayed, which is the rationale for public-access AED programs and rapid AED use.

questions.question: Which population is served by a separate chain introduced in the 2025 Guidelines?
options: A. Geriatric patients
B. Newborns (Neonatal Chain of Care)
C. Trauma patients
D. Pregnant patients
questions.rationale: The 2025 Guidelines introduced a distinct Neonatal Chain of Care for newborns, separate from the universal Chain of Survival used for other populations.

questions.question: A patient regains a pulse (ROSC) after CPR and defibrillation. What is the correct BLS mindset?
options: A. The resuscitation is complete and no further care is needed
B. Continue monitoring and support post-arrest care; recovery is part of the chain
C. Remove the AED pads immediately and reassure the family it is over
D. Stop reassessing because the pulse has returned
questions.rationale: ROSC is not the finish line. Post-cardiac arrest care (link 5) and recovery (link 6) follow; the patient still needs close monitoring, support, and continued care.

questions.question: Which statement about defibrillation and BLS scope is TRUE?
options: A. Defibrillation with an AED is exclusively an advanced-provider skill
B. AED use is a core BLS and public-access action available to lay rescuers
C. Only physicians may apply an AED
D. AEDs should only be used after epinephrine is given
questions.rationale: AED use is intentionally designed for lay rescuers and BLS providers. Public-access defibrillation is a cornerstone of the chain and does not require advanced training.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of continuous quality improvement within a system of care?
options: A. To assign blame after unsuccessful resuscitations
B. To measure resuscitation performance and feed it back so future outcomes improve
C. To reduce the number of AEDs in public places
D. To replace bystander CPR with technology
questions.rationale: Quality improvement measures performance (such as compression quality and time to defibrillation) and feeds it back to responders and the system, strengthening the chain and improving future survival.

questions.question: Link 4 of the universal chain, advanced resuscitation interventions, includes which of the following?
options: A. Recognizing the arrest
B. Airway management, medications, and treating reversible causes by trained providers
C. Applying the first AED shock
D. Coaching a bystander over the phone
questions.rationale: Advanced resuscitation interventions build on high-quality CPR and defibrillation, adding advanced airway management, medications, and treatment of reversible causes delivered by trained providers.

questions.question: Which sequence best represents a BLS provider's priorities when finding an unresponsive, pulseless adult?
options: A. Start an IV, then call for help, then compress
B. Activate emergency response and get an AED, start high-quality CPR, defibrillate when available
C. Perform a detailed head-to-toe exam before compressions
D. Wait for advanced providers before doing anything
questions.rationale: The BLS priority sequence is to activate the emergency response and obtain an AED, begin high-quality compressions immediately, and defibrillate as soon as the AED is available — the front links of the chain.

questions.question: Why did the AHA move from multiple chains to a single universal Chain of Survival in 2025?
options: A. To make resuscitation harder to teach
B. To simplify and unify the concept across ages and settings while keeping setting-specific emphasis
C. Because defibrillation is no longer recommended
D. To eliminate the role of bystanders
questions.rationale: A single universal chain simplifies teaching and unifies the core sequence across adult, pediatric, in-hospital, and out-of-hospital arrest, while the emphasis (e.g., prevention in hospitals vs. bystander action in the community) still varies by setting.

questions.question: A hospital rapid-response team is called for a deteriorating patient before any arrest occurs. Which link/emphasis does this represent?
options: A. Recovery
B. Surveillance and prevention (IHCA emphasis)
C. Defibrillation
D. Post-cardiac arrest care
questions.rationale: Rapid-response and early-warning systems reflect the surveillance-and-prevention emphasis of the in-hospital setting, aiming to prevent arrest by recognizing and treating deterioration early.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes why public-access AED placement is emphasized?
options: A. AEDs replace the need for CPR
B. Rapid defibrillation before EMS arrival improves survival in shockable arrests
C. AEDs are only useful in hospitals
D. Public AEDs are meant for training, not real use
questions.rationale: Because survival in shockable rhythms declines with each minute of delay, placing AEDs where the public can reach them enables defibrillation before EMS arrives, strengthening a critical link.

questions.question: The recovery link implies that good resuscitation practice ALSO includes:
options: A. Stopping all follow-up once ROSC is achieved
B. Supporting survivors and families and debriefing rescuers
C. Avoiding any discussion of the event with the family
D. Immediately discharging the patient
questions.rationale: The recovery link reframes survival as an ongoing journey, so supporting survivors and families and debriefing the rescue team are part of complete, high-quality resuscitation care.

questions.question: Which statement about the relationship between links is MOST accurate?
options: A. Links can be skipped without affecting outcome
B. Each link builds on and depends upon the previous links
C. The last link is the only one that matters
D. Bystander links are unimportant if EMS is nearby
questions.rationale: The chain is sequential and interdependent: each link builds on the ones before it, so timely, high-quality performance of the early links sets up the success of the later ones.
